text: ClearPath's current onboarding takes ~6 weeks before a new consultant can work independently, and leadership wants it below 3 weeks.
Interim observation found the actual time to meaningful autonomy is closer to 6.5-7 weeks, longer than the official six-week figure.
Meridian recommends a 4-week onboarding target (achievable with existing team plus standardized curriculum) rather than the board's 3-week goal.
The final report sets the onboarding target at 3-4 weeks via a formalized playbook, curriculum, and automated tool provisioning.
How long does a report actually spend in the audit queue? Team leads' estimates vary by a factor of three with no objective measurement.
Audit cycle-time estimates conflict: Marco Fiore 3-4 days, Laura Galli 6-8 days, a junior auditor 2-3 days; no tracking exists to reconcile them.
During intake observation Marco Fiore stated he was managing 3 active clients simultaneously.
In the divergent intake note Marco reported managing 5 concurrent clients.
Priya Anand's quarterly capacity tracking records Marco at 3 clients for Q3 2025, contradicting his self-reported figure of 5.
Junior-consultant turnover is approximately 18% annually, higher than the sector average.
The 18% turnover figure was never verified during the engagement yet underpins two recommendations (organizational structure and audit efficiency).
Should the review include the Milan satellite office, which was not mentioned in the SOW?
Meridian will conduct a light-touch Milan assessment as a report appendix, keeping the December 1 delivery and fees intact rather than a full deep-dive.
The Milan satellite assessment will be included as an appendix in the December 1 final deliverable.
Nora flags that ClearPath's intake process is significantly worse than internal expectation and should become a major recommendation.
At midpoint the intake bottleneck is confirmed as real and significant with solid data, set to be the lead finding.
An observed client intake took 9 days end-to-end against Marco's team target of 3-5 days.
Nora proposes resequencing the workplan to front-load the intake analysis and let audit-trail work take weeks 8-9 once data is complete.
Marcus approves the resequencing: intake findings lead, audit analysis follows with caveats, and reporting-analysis depth is scaled back.
David Park is difficult to schedule, gating the system-architecture detail needed before the audit-trail methodology can be finalized.
The Q3 2024 compliance database migration created historical data gaps that complicate audit-trail tracing.
Yuki is not fully confident in severity ratings for risks #7 and #9 (documentation standards) because the data samples are smaller than preferred.
On Elena Martinelli's first day, IT provisioning was incomplete: database access still pending and only a slow loaner laptop available, blocking substantive work.
A consultant assembled a client report by copy-pasting ~60% from a prior report and using a newer template found via email rather than the 2022 shared-drive version.
ClearPath's internal Confluence wiki was last updated March 2023 and consultants do not trust it, defaulting to Slack and external contacts.
What is ClearPath's actual team-leader structure? Observation identified 6 leaders, conflicting with the org matrix and earlier discussion.
Meridian recommends a two-pass audit review (peer preliminary review plus senior spot-check) to cut the queue to 2-3 days.
The final report states the audit cycle-time target as 3-4 days, slightly higher than the 2-3 days previewed.
Meridian recommends a dedicated operations-excellence coordinator role, with Riccardo Boni suggested as owner.
Marcus plans to lead the board presentation with the intake findings, middle-ground the audit infrastructure work, and hedge on documentation standardization.
ClearPath's team utilization is 68% versus a 72-80% peer/industry benchmark.
The SOW's data-access clause grants rights to only 12 months of historical logs, a boundary flagged as likely to be tested early.
